description: The vast body of saltwater covering most of Earth's surface, connecting all continents. 'Thaluro' names the great waters that encircle the world—source of weather, holder of depths unknown, the ultimate commons shared by all lands.
sound_symbolism: The word opens to boundless expanse. 'Tha' /θa/ begins with ancient breath and primordial depth. 'Lu' flows through the ocean's liquid vastness, and 'ro' holds its enduring presence out toward the horizon.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun. Plural with 'lo': 'lo thaluro' (oceans). An ocean is a place and body of water rather than a detached or crafted object, so it has no canonical classifier. Distinguished from 'moriwhe' (sea) by scale—ocean is global and boundless, sea is regional or bounded. Often used for the concept of ultimate depth or source.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The ocean teaches boundlessness—no matter how many waves arise, the ocean itself remains whole and inexhaustible.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The ocean was the great mystery and connector—source of life, feared and revered, crossed by brave voyagers who understood its power.
